key_departures.impact: Traded to Titans for DT T'Vondre Sweat (Feb. 26 / officially Mar. 12). Johnson had 3 sacks in 2025 on his fifth-year option ($13.4M), declining from a 7.5-sack Pro Bowl year in 2023. The trade changed the position, not eliminated the need — EDGE depth was immediately required.
Started all 17 games at guard in 2025. Signed 3yr/$30M with Baltimore. Departure alongside Vera-Tucker leaves a significant void at interior OL.
Signed a 3yr/$42M deal ($21M guaranteed) with the Patriots. Former first-round pick. Loss of both starting guards in the same free agency period creates an acute IOL need.
Signed 2yr/$17M with Cleveland. Former All-Pro (2023) who declined in 2025 (83 tackles, scheme misfit). Departure partially mitigated by Davis signing, but still a notable hole at LB depth.
Signed 3yr/$17.5M with Colts. Was a rotational edge rusher (8.5 sacks in Jets career). Departure was expected given new free agent additions. Depth loss at EDGE, not a starter hole.
Led NFL in field-goal percentage for three straight seasons. Departure creates a kicker vacancy — Jets now adding Cade York as a replacement candidate.

signings.trade_details: Jets sent 2026 7th-round pick (via Chargers) to Dolphins

contract.guaranteed_note: Guarantee structure not fully disclosed; AAV $13.33M on 3yr/$40M extension

signings.analysis: Five-time Pro Bowler and three-time All-Pro acquired for a seventh-round pick. Three-year commitment at age 29 at $13.33M AAV is solidly in the mid-to-upper starter tier at safety. The Jets had zero interceptions as a team in 2025 — Fitzpatrick brings immediate ball-hawking capability, leadership, and a starting-caliber floor. Safety goes from a critical need to a strength.

Three-year deal with $22.5M guaranteed signals a real investment. Ossai, 25, had 5 sacks in each of the last two seasons and career highs in pressure rate and pass-rush win rate in 2025. He projects as a starter in the Jets' new 3-4 front. At $12M AAV he is mid-to-low-end starter money — not elite, but meaningful. With Will McDonald IV also returning, Ossai partially addresses the EDGE need, but production (5 sacks/year) is modest and the Jets still lack a dominant pass rusher. EDGE need drops but remains — they should still target EDGE in the draft (No. 2 overall is firmly in play).

One-year deal at $10M fully guaranteed. Enagbare is a durable three-down player but a borderline starter — 2.5 sacks in 2025 and career best of 4.0. Known primarily as a run defender. One-year structure signals a prove-it deal, not a solution. Adds quality depth and run-defense value but does not resolve the EDGE need long-term. Combined with Ossai, the depth is improved but elite pass-rushing remains absent.

Two-year deal with $15M fully guaranteed is a real commitment, even for a 37-year-old. Davis set a career-high 143 tackles in 2025 (81.4 PFF grade) and brings elite leadership and run-defense capability. HC Aaron Glenn knows him from three seasons together in New Orleans. Davis replaces Quincy Williams with a significant upgrade in scheme fit and likely in production, allowing Jamien Sherwood to shift to his natural WILL role. However, the age (37, Year 15) and two-year window mean LB still carries moderate long-term risk. Need is partially addressed for 2026 but remains a long-term consideration.

One-year, $10.5M deal with $9.65M guaranteed. Onyemata is a run-stuffing DT (career-high run stop win rate in 2025, 62 tackles, 1 sack starting 17 games for ATL). Glenn coached him for five years in New Orleans. One-year structure limits the commitment, but combined with T'Vondre Sweat (trade), Harrison Phillips, and Jowon Briggs, the interior is substantially upgraded. The Jets finished 29th in run defense in 2025 — this group addresses that. IDL need meaningfully reduced for 2026.

signings.trade_details: Jets sent EDGE Jermaine Johnson II to Titans; Sweat has 2 years remaining on rookie deal at ~$1.7M cap hit

contract.guaranteed_note: Remaining rookie contract; signed through 2027 at ~$1.7M cap hit in 2026

signings.analysis: Exceptional value acquisition. Sweat (6-4, 366 lbs.) is PFF's 5th-best IDL and 4th-best run defender in 2025. Still on his rookie deal through 2027 at a trivial cap number (~$1.7M in 2026). The centerpiece of the Jets' new 3-4 nose tackle alignment. Paired with Onyemata, Harrison Phillips, and Jowon Briggs, the interior defensive line is now well-stocked. IDL transitions from a need to a strength for 2026.

signings.trade_details: Jets sent 2026 6th-round pick (#208) to Raiders; received Smith and 2026 7th-round pick (#228). Raiders pay $16.2M of Smith's salary; Jets pay $3.3M.
signings.analysis: Extraordinary cap efficiency — the Jets pay just $3.3M of Smith's total $19.5M salary, with the Raiders covering $16.2M. One-year restructured deal only. Smith (35) is a veteran bridge at best; he went 15 games for the Raiders with 19 TDs/17 INTs in a disastrous 2025 campaign. The one-year structure is the tell: this is a bridge, not a solution. QB remains a critical long-term need and the Jets are expected to address it in the draft (No. 2 pick). This partially addresses the 2026 season need only.

One-year deal worth up to $6M, with $3.5M guaranteed. Belton competes for the SS2 role alongside Fitzpatrick. He had 120 tackles and 1 INT for the Giants in 2025 and led all safeties in tackling efficiency (95%). Excellent special teams player. Provides quality depth behind Fitzpatrick — does not address any unmet need but solidifies the safety room. Good value signing.

Bargain signing. Wright led all CBs tied for the league lead with 5 INTs in 2025 — ironic given the Jets infamously had zero team INTs. Fully guaranteed one-year deal at $3.5M (up to $5.5M with incentives) for a Pro Bowl selection is exceptional value. He is inconsistent in coverage and was on a Vikings practice squad a year before his breakout. One-year deal signals this is an opportunistic depth signing, not a long-term CB solution. CB remains a need for the draft, though Wright could win a starting job. Need drops slightly but is not resolved.

Two-year deal with $7.5M guaranteed addresses the immediate LG vacancy left by Simpson and Vera-Tucker. Parham, 26, has 63 starts and posted a career-high pass-block win rate in 2025 despite playing on the Raiders' historically bad OL (league-high 64 sacks). At $8M AAV he is firmly mid-tier/depth money. Two-year deal with modest guarantees signals a bridge starter. ESPN and team sources explicitly note they will still look to fortify IOL in the draft. Need partially addressed — IOL remains a draft target.

Two-year deal worth up to $9M with $3.5M guaranteed. Depth/backup center signing. Provides interior versatility and competition. Does not resolve the starting IOL need, but improves the overall depth of the group after losing two starters. Low-impact signing in terms of need resolution.

contract.guaranteed_note: Contract terms not yet reported; re-signed per NFL.com/team announcement

signings.analysis: Re-signed as a depth/rotational TE. Woods is a big-bodied receiving tight end (6-7). Terms not yet publicly reported. Retaining him maintains continuity at TE behind Jeremy Ruckert (already under contract extension). No new need addressed; merely maintains existing roster depth.

Non-exclusive franchise tag ($14.293M, fully guaranteed). Hall rushed for 1,065 yards in 2025 — first Jets RB over 1,000 since 2015. Tag prevents him from testing free agency while long-term extension negotiations continue (deadline July 15). RB is not a need for the Jets in 2026 as a result. One-year tag does not fully lock in the future, but for draft purposes, RB is off the board as a priority.

One-year, $1.5M fully guaranteed re-sign. Beck is a fullback who played a role in both offense and special teams in 2025 under new OC Frank Reich, who clearly has plans for him. Low-cost continuity move. No need impact.

contract.guaranteed_note: Terms not fully reported; one-year deal per sources

signings.analysis: Re-signed as the Jets' swing tackle/sixth offensive lineman. Mitchell has 17 career starts and versatility at G/T. Retaining him maintains OL depth continuity entering 2026. No need impact — OT was not a pre-FA need and this signing does not create or resolve any tier-relevant need.

contract.guaranteed_note: Terms not publicly reported; one-year deal per sources

signings.analysis: Re-signed on a one-year deal. Walker graded as PFF's 8th-best LB in Weeks 14-18 in 2025 (min. 130 snaps) and played 81% of special teams snaps. Depth LB re-sign that pairs with Demario Davis. Minimal impact on need tier — LB depth is improved but Davis is the only meaningful starter.

contract.guaranteed_note: Terms not publicly reported; one-year deal per sources

signings.analysis: Signed after Nick Folk departed to Atlanta. York is a career 73% field-goal kicker and a former fourth-round pick (Browns, 2022). He last appeared in an NFL game in 2024. This is a training camp competition signing, not a solution. The kicker need created by Folk's departure remains real — York is unproven at this level.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Minkah Fitzpatrick trade (3yr/$40M) + Dane Belton signing completely resolves the safety room. Fitzpatrick is a 5x Pro Bowler still playing at elite level (81.8 PFF grade in 2025). Safety is now a team strength. No draft capital needed here.
Breece Hall franchise tag ($14.293M, fully guaranteed) retains the starter through 2026. Hall rushed for 1,065 yards in 2025. Long-term deal discussions ongoing. RB is not a draft need in 2026.

T'Vondre Sweat trade (2 years remaining on rookie deal, PFF's 5th-best IDL) plus David Onyemata signing (1yr/$10.5M) plus Harrison Phillips and Jowon Briggs already on roster creates a very solid interior. IDL goes from a meaningful need to a luxury pick at best. No draft priority needed.

Demario Davis (2yr/$22M, $15M guaranteed) is a meaningful upgrade over Quincy Williams and provides veteran leadership. However, Davis is 37 and on a short effective window. Mykal Walker re-signed provides depth. LB drops one tier — still worth targeting in mid-rounds for long-term succession planning.

Joseph Ossai (3yr/$36M, $22.5M gtd) plus Kingsley Enagbare (1yr/$10M) plus Will McDonald IV give the Jets three viable edge players. But Ossai averages only 5 sacks/year, Enagbare is a prove-it one-year deal, and McDonald is still developing. The Jets recorded just 26 sacks as a team in 2025 — the pass rush is improved but not elite. EDGE is still a draft priority at No. 2 overall and should remain a Tier 2 need.

Dylan Parham (2yr/$16M, $7.5M gtd) fills the starting LG slot vacated by Simpson and Vera-Tucker. Austin Schlottmann adds backup depth. However, losing both starting guards to free agency is significant, Parham comes from a historically bad OL in Las Vegas, and the team explicitly plans to add IOL in the draft. Need drops one tier but remains a meaningful target in rounds 2-4.

Nahshon Wright (1yr/$3.5M fully gtd) is excellent value but a one-year, prove-it deal for an inconsistent player who had one breakout season. The Jets had zero INTs as a team in 2025 and still lack a lockdown CB1. With Sauce Gardner gone (traded to Colts in 2025), the CB room is thin outside of 2025 third-rounder Azareye'h Thomas. CB remains a Tier 2 draft need.

Geno Smith trade (1yr/$3.3M Jets portion) provides a veteran bridge for 2026 at near-minimum cost. However, Smith is 35 and on a one-year deal — the Jets' long-term QB need is completely unresolved. At No. 2 overall, QB could still be taken. Drops from Tier 1 (urgent starter need) to Tier 2 (bridge in place, but franchise QB still required through draft).

No meaningful WR additions in free agency. Garrett Wilson is under contract (extension) but a true WR2/WR3 remains absent from the roster. WR need is unchanged and should be addressed in the draft (rounds 2-4).

Nick Folk (3x NFL leader in FG% departed to Falcons) creates a new need. Cade York signed on a one-year deal is unproven (career 73% FG%). Kicker is now a legitimate concern — should be addressed via competition or draft/UDFA.

key_additions.headline: Five-time Pro Bowler and three-time All-Pro acquired for a 7th-round pick to anchor a secondary that had zero INTs in 2025

key_additions.contract_summary: Trade (Jermaine Johnson II to TEN); rookie deal through 2027 (~$1.7M cap hit)
key_additions.headline: PFF's 5th-best interior defender acquired on rookie deal to anchor the Jets' new 3-4 front as nose tackle
25-year-old ascending edge rusher (5 sacks in each of last 2 seasons) brings youth and a three-year commitment to a depleted pass rush
37-year-old linebacker returns for 3rd Jets stint, bringing elite run defense and veteran leadership to Aaron Glenn's revamped defense
Bridge starter acquired at near-minimum cost with Raiders covering $16.2M of his salary; returns to the franchise that drafted him in 2013
26-year-old veteran guard (63 career starts) fills starting vacancy after loss of both Simpson and Vera-Tucker
League co-leader in INTs in 2025 (5) signed for virtually nothing to address a secondary that was historically turnover-starved
33-year-old run-stuffing DT (career-high run stop win rate in 2025) reunites with Aaron Glenn to fortify a run defense that ranked 29th in 2025

key_losses.headline: Former first-round EDGE traded away in savvy value swap, opening a starter-level hole at edge rusher that the draft (No. 2 pick) is expected to fill
Former first-round pick departs to New England on a $42M deal, leaving the Jets without both starting guards entering 2026
Durable starting guard (34 straight starts for NYJ) signs with the Ravens at above-market value, compounding the interior OL void
Former All-Pro LB departs after falling out with the coaching staff, replaced by veteran Demario Davis in Aaron Glenn's defensive overhaul
Three-time NFL field-goal percentage leader departs, leaving the Jets with an unresolved kicker competition entering 2026

notes: Contract details sourced from ESPN, NFL.com, Spotrac, OverTheCap, and credentialed reporters (Schefter, Rapoport, Pelissero, Garafolo). Ossai contract reported as both $34.5M (ESPN/AOL) and $36M (AP/TSN) — using $36M per AP/TSN and multiple reports. Dylan Parham contract reported as up to $20M by Rapoport/NFL.com but base confirmed at 2yr/$16M per ESPN. Jelani Woods and Max Mitchell re-sign terms not yet fully publicly reported. Austin Schlottmann contract sourced from NFL.com (2yr/$9M, $3.5M gtd). Minkah Fitzpatrick guarantee structure not fully disclosed. Cap space post-FA sourced from OverTheCap ($37,124,446 remaining as of March 13, 2026).
